rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending immediate-versus-delayed promotion research, and the cited 2011 JMRK paper directly examines instant vs delayed mail-in rebate choices and willingness to wait in intertemporal consumer decisions. This strong construct/task overlap (promotion timing, delay of rewards, present-bias/self-control mechanisms) makes the citation plausibly foundational or theory-relevant rather than an obvious incidental mention.
